channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-18
item.description:
The open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the **scienceverse/metacheck** and **JamieCummins/regcheck** projects have both made strides in improving their tools, with bug fixes and enhancements to their reporting and storage mechanisms, respectively. Meanwhile, **giladfeldman/docpluck** has shipped version 2.4.92 and made notable changes to its documentation and rendering features. The **Davidvandijcke/coarse** project has made impressive gains in localization, adding features such as a manual language switcher and auto-detection. These developments demonstrate the community's commitment to collaboration and continuous improvement, with many projects tackling complex issues and pushing the boundaries of what's possible. As these projects continue to evolve, we can expect to see even more exciting innovations and connections between them.

112 commits across 7 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck33 commits
docpluck32 commits
metacheck27 commits
coarse17 commits
escicheck1 commits

item.title: metacheck: 4 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The 'dev' branch received a fix where `create_report_app()` was changed to `report_app()` in the Get Started vignette, as stated by Daniel Lakens. On the 'ethics_check' branch, Lakens added an ethics check and also fixed a bug. The 'new_modules' branch saw a revert of the fix for app instruction #314, only to then be immediately re-applied by Lakens, effectively fixing the app instruction #314. Additionally, the 'new_modules' branch had these changes made by Lakens in quick succession, suggesting a trial-and-error approach to resolving the issue with app instruction #314.

4 commit(s) today:

  • 73736b0 [dev] Fix create_report_app() to report_app() in Get Started vignette — Daniel Lakens
  • 00d7953 [ethics_check] add ethics_check + bug fix — Lakens
  • 2b8a367 [new_modules] Revert "fix app instruction #314" — Lakens
  • 1d3719b [new_modules] fix app instruction #314 — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 6 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with three commits made to it. The first commit, "docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary)", indicates that a handoff was run, version 2.4.92 was shipped, the caption guard was reverted, and work was paused at an architectural boundary. Another commit, "docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal", added documentation for cycle 2, which includes the affiliation guard for version 2.4.92, and cycle 3, where the caption-follows feature was reverted. A feature commit, "feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92)", changed the rendering to never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings, specifically for version 2.4.92.

6 commit(s) today:

  • 2fd8711 [main] docs(iterate): 2026-06-18 run handoff (v2.4.92 shipped, caption-guard reverted, paused at architectural boundary) — Gilad Feldman
  • c671929 [main] docs(iterate): cycle 2 (v2.4.92 affiliation guard) + cycle 3 (caption-follows REVERTED) journal — Gilad Feldman
  • cf420ca [main] feat(render): never promote affiliation lines to subsection headings (v2.4.92) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: coarse: 16 commits today
item.description:
Davidvandijcke/coarse

Work was done on the 'dev' branch, as well as feature branches 'feat/i18n-status-review-pages', 'feat/231-site-i18n', 'feat/229-i18n-labels', and 'feat/226-i18n-deenglish'. The 'feat(i18n): localize status + review pages' feature was added, which includes localizing the status and review pages, and an Opus QA of all 12 catalogs was performed. The website UI was localized with a manual language switcher, as stated in the commit message "feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H)". Review headers and completion email were also localized, with the commit message specifying "feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E)". Additionally, the analysis was "de-English-ified" and auto-detection, web language picker, and RTL render were implemented, with the commit message noting "feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2)". The 'feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker' feature was added to the 'dev' branch, which adds GLM 5.2 to the model picker.

16 commit(s) today:

  • 9ac6256 [feat/i18n-status-review-pages] feat(i18n): localize status + review pages; Opus QA of all 12 catalogs (#233) — Davidvandijcke
  • 8faa90a [dev] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) (#232) — David Van Dijcke
  • 395a58c [feat/231-site-i18n] feat(i18n): localized website UI with manual language switcher (PR-H) (#231) — Davidvandijcke
  • eae44c1 [dev] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) (#230) — David Van Dijcke
  • db35eb5 [feat/229-i18n-labels] feat(i18n): localized review headers + completion email (PR-E) (#229) — Davidvandijcke
  • ef7b339 [dev] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) (#228) — David Van Dijcke
  • 3117681 [dev] feat(i18n): auto-detection + web language picker + RTL render (PR-B2) (#223) (#225) — David Van Dijcke
  • 8510764 [feat/226-i18n-deenglish] feat(i18n): de-English-ify the analysis (PR-C) (#226) — Davidvandijcke
  • a12f9dc [dev] feat(web): add GLM 5.2 to the model picker (#227) — David Van Dijcke

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 14 commits today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The staging branch was the focus of the recent work, with multiple commits made to it. The first commit "harden json handling" suggests that the JSON handling in the repository was made more robust. A fix was then applied to address an "oss bug", as stated in the corresponding commit message. Additionally, the storage mechanism was modified to "use more efficient storage", increasing the overall performance. Finally, a patch was applied to the "oss SDK handler" to resolve any existing issues with it.

14 commit(s) today:

  • 51b9ba6 [staging] harden json handling — Jamie Cummins
  • 6cdde08 [staging] fix oss bug — Jamie Cummins
  • 1fd2de7 [staging] use more efficient storage — Jamie Cummins
  • 4b7a527 [staging] patch oss SDK handler — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

Work was done on the 'main' branch, where several sync operations were performed, including syncing from escicheckapp@f52d7ce for effectcheck v0.6.3, escicheckapp@e060e98 for effectcheck v0.6.2, escicheckapp@5093197 for effectcheck v0.6.1, and escicheckapp@1ebe05f for effectcheck v0.5.18. A temporary branch 'sync/20260525-150441-b801333' was also used for a sync operation from escicheckapp@b801333 for effectcheck v0.6.2. The commit messages specifically mention syncing from various escicheckapp versions, as stated in the messages, such as "sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3)". The sync operations were performed by both Gilad Feldman and github-actions[bot].

1 commit(s) today:

  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a new commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned in the provided commit messages. The commit message directly mentions the addition of the Chrome Webstore link, indicating this was the sole purpose of the commit. There are no other changes or additions to report from the given commits.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
